_comment: Declarative E2E fixture dataset. Every agent name starts with 'e2e-test-' so the Playwright suite can discriminate its own fixtures amid any other data in the shared dev DB. session_ids are derived deterministically in seed.py via uuid5(NAMESPACE_FLIGHTDECK, 'flightdeck-e2e/<agent_name>/<role>'), so each seed run addresses the same session rows and idempotency is a per-session HTTP GET.

session_roles._comment: Timeline offsets drive the UI state each fixture tests. Offsets are seconds relative to seed-time. 'aged-closed' and 'stale' require a post-seed SQL backdate because the worker stamps last_seen_at = NOW() regardless of event payload timestamp; see seed.py :: backdate_sessions. 'phase4_extras' (when present) is a list of additional event-shape strings the seeder emits on top of the base session_start / pre_call / post_call / tool_call / session_end timeline. Recognised values: 'embeddings', 'streaming_post_call', 'streaming_post_call_aborted', 'llm_error_rate_limit', 'llm_error_context_overflow', 'llm_error_authentication', 'llm_error_timeout'.

fresh-active.purpose: T1 LIVE/RECENT bucket; T5 visible in swimlane; T14 embeddings drawer row (Phase 4 polish); T15 happy-path streaming post_call (Phase 4 polish)

recent-closed.purpose: T1 RECENT bucket; T5 closed-state badge; T16 carries one llm_error event so the session row's red error-indicator dot appears alongside the closed badge

aged-closed.purpose: T5 anchor: started 28h ago, ended 27h ago. Must NOT appear in 24h swimlane circles (server filters /v1/sessions by started_at AT fleet.ts::SWIMLANE_LOOKBACK_MS=24h) but MUST appear in expanded drawer via loadExpandedSessions (which fetches with no from/to bound). Plan called for 23h/22h but those values sit INSIDE the 24h window; 28h/27h gives a safe margin either side of the boundary. Requires post-seed SQL backdate AND force_state='closed'.

stale.purpose: T1 non-active state. 3h gap puts it past both the 2-min stale and 10-min lost reconciler thresholds (see workers/internal/writer/postgres.go:543). seed.py pins state='lost' deterministically (matches what the reconciler would set on its next pass) so a freshly-seeded stack is test-stable without waiting for a reconciler tick. Requires post-seed SQL backdate + force_state.

error-active.purpose: T16 anchor: a single live session that emits four distinct llm_error taxonomy values (rate_limit, context_overflow, authentication, timeout) plus an aborted streaming post_call. Drives the ERROR TYPE facet on Investigate (4 distinct pills) and the session-row red dot tooltip (4 distinct values listed). Coding agent only -- the other agents stay clean so T16 can assert the dot is selectively applied.
